lines: This is MPV, the media player. But it's never looked like this. Frosted glass controls, spring animations, Apple's Liquid Glass design, completely free. Let me show you.
If you've ever opened a really big video and watched your player stutter, freeze, or just give up, you know the feeling.
I've used them all. VLC, KMPlayer, the usual players. And for everyday clips, they're perfectly fine.
But push them. A ten gigabyte movie, an eighteen gigabyte remux, and they start to lag and hang. In my experience, MPV just, didn't. It stayed buttery smooth, every single time.
Same story with big playlists. Hundreds of small clips queued up, and the others crawled. MPV handled it without breaking a sweat.
That's because MPV is lean, and GPU accelerated at its core. It spends its energy playing your video, not fighting it.
So MPV won me over on performance. There was just one problem. It always looked a little, plain. Not anymore.

This is MPV Liquid Glass, a skin built on top of you-osc. Pure Lua, no compiling, no extra software. If you can copy and paste a folder, you can install it. There are two parts. First we install MPV, then we drop the skin in.
And here's what you unlock. A full width glass progress bar with chapter markers, and every control just one glance away.
Scroll over the speed button, and an elastic speedometer springs to life.
It even reshapes itself for portrait and reels sized video. And hit Control T any time to switch to a clean light theme.

That's it. If this earned a spot in your setup, give the repo a star. Want a new feature, or found a bug? It's all on the page, and there's even a guide for contributors. The link is in the description. Thanks for watching.
